config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama agar kepentingan masyarakat tidak berubah menjadi alasan untuk menekan kebebasan, martabat, atau perlindungan orang lemah. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat keseimbangan, bukan dengan mengorbankan hak dasar seseorang. Jika prioritas selalu jatuh ke masyarakat di atas individu, penyalahgunaan kuasa jadi lebih mudah dibenarkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States we have seen how easily "the common good" can be used to excuse abuse, discrimination, or government overreach. That said, individual rights are not absolute in every case, since public health, safety, and basic order do matter. The real question is whether a restriction is narrowly necessary and fair, not whether society gets a blank check over the person. If we reverse the burden and let society outrank the individual by default, I think we weaken the very rights that protect minorities and unpopular people.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju kalau kepentingan masyarakat dijadikan prioritas di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, sementara orang kecil yang paling dulu menanggung akibatnya. Memang ada situasi darurat seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, tetapi pembatasannya harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa diuji, bukan jadi cek kosong untuk negara atau mayoritas. Jadi, yang benar menurut saya bukan masyarakat selalu di atas individu, melainkan hak individu menjadi batas yang menjaga agar kepentingan sosial tidak berubah menjadi alat penindasan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From a United States perspective, I am very wary of any argument that gives the majority or the government a default override, because history here shows how often that language has been used to justify discrimination, censorship, and abuse. Public health and safety can justify limited restrictions, but only when they are narrow, necessary, and accountable; otherwise, “society first” becomes a convenient excuse to trample minorities and unpopular people. To me, individual rights are not a luxury that society grants when convenient — they are the guardrails that keep society from becoming oppressive.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dalam konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang tidak proporsional, dan yang paling sering kena justru orang kecil atau kelompok minoritas. Memang ada keadaan darurat seperti wabah atau ancaman keamanan, tetapi pembatasannya harus ketat, sementara, dan bisa diawasi, bukan jadi dalih permanen. Jadi menurut saya, hak individu harus menjadi pagar utama, karena tanpa itu kepentingan sosial gampang berubah menjadi alat tekanan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the individual as a general principle. From a U.S. perspective, I am especially suspicious of “society first” language because it has too often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, even when officials claim noble motives. Yes, public health and safety can justify limited restrictions, but those limits have to be narrow, temporary, and accountable, not a default license to override people’s rights. To me, individual rights are the boundary that keeps the common good from turning into majority rule without restraint.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
